name: Jayatirtha
dates_notes: Pontifical succession records; dated by manuscript and citation.
core_thesis:
Jayatīrtha (called *Ṭīkācārya*, "Master of Sub-Commentary") is the systematiser through whom Madhva's *Tattvavāda* becomes a fully scholastic *darśana*. He introduces no new doctrine. What he supplies is dialectical apparatus: each Madhva position is restated in formal *pramāṇa*-and-*anumāna* form, each contested *adhikaraṇa* of the *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* and each contested *kārikā* of the *Anuvyākhyāna* is fixed against alternative construals, and the school's standard answers to its principal opponents are engineered.

Four interpretive choices govern the corpus. First, *bheda* (difference) is *svarūpa-svabhāva*, constitutive of the natures of the relata, and not an additional entity attached to those natures (*Nyāya-Sudhā* on [*Anuvyākhyāna* 1.4.111–112](cite://madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.4.111-112); [*Vādāvalī* §§1–4](cite://jayatirtha/vadavali/1-4)). The standard regress-objection ("if *A* differs from *B*, a further *bheda* is needed to differentiate *A*'s *bheda* from *B*'s *bheda*, and so on") fails because it presupposes that *bheda* is the kind of item that needs further differentiation; on the *svarūpa-svabhāva* construal it is not ([*Vādāvalī* §3](cite://jayatirtha/vadavali/3), against the Nyāya *padārtha*-construal).

Second, *viśeṣa* is a self-distinguishing relational element that grounds intra-substantial differentiation without numerical multiplication of substances. Where Madhva had introduced the device (at *Tattva-Saṅkhyāna* and [*Anuvyākhyāna* 2.3](cite://madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3) *adhyāya*), Jayatīrtha specifies the conditions under which it can be appealed to and the inferences in which it can do work (*Nyāya-Sudhā* on [*Anuvyākhyāna* 2.3](cite://madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3), the *viśeṣa-vāda* discussion).

Third, *sākṣi* (witness-consciousness) is a *pramāṇa* in its own right; its verdicts are not subject to *bādha* (sublation) by mediated cognition (*Nyāya-Sudhā* on [*Anuvyākhyāna* 3.2](cite://madhva/anuvyakhyana/3.2) ff., the *sākṣi-pramāṇa* section). The asymmetry runs in one direction only: inference may be corrected by *sākṣi*, but not the reverse.

Fourth, the *jīva*'s relation to Viṣṇu as *bimba-pratibimba* (original-and-image) is asymmetric (*Tattva-Prakāśikā* on *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* 2.3.43, the *aṃśa-adhikaraṇa* with the *ghaṭākāśa* analogy; *Nyāya-Sudhā* on [*Anuvyākhyāna* 2.3.43–44](cite://madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.43-44)). The image owes everything to the original; the original owes nothing to the image. Reciprocity here is excluded (the position fixed against Bhāskara's reciprocal *aupādhika* construal at [*Vādāvalī* §5](cite://jayatirtha/vadavali/5)).

Vyāsatīrtha's *Nyāyāmṛta* and Rāghavendra's *Pariṃala* both presuppose this scaffolding.

engaged_works.title: Nyaya-Sudha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Madhva's Anuvyākhyāna.
engaged_works.summary:
Jayatīrtha's long prose commentary on Madhva's *Anuvyākhyāna*. Together the two texts constitute the spinal scholastic apparatus of the school; for the later tradition, the *Sudhā* rather than the *Anuvyākhyāna* itself is the base-text of technical exegesis.

The method is verse by verse across all four *adhyāyas*. For each *kārikā*, Jayatīrtha reconstructs Madhva's compressed argument as a formal *anumāna*: *pakṣa* (subject), *sādhya* (predicate-to-be-established), *hetu* (reason), *vyāpti* (universal concomitance), *udāharaṇa* (illustration), and *upanaya–nigamana* (application and conclusion). Each *pūrvapakṣa* (Advaitic, Bhāskarīya, Naiyāyika, Buddhist, Mīmāṃsaka) is identified and answered in turn. Where the *Bhāṣya* admits more than one construal, the *Sudhā* fixes the school's reading.

Four technical achievements are concentrated here. *Bheda* is established as *svarūpa-svabhāva* (*Nyāya-Sudhā* on [*Anuvyākhyāna* 1.4.111–112](cite://madhva/anuvyakhyana/1.4.111-112)), blocking the standard regress-objection at the level of the *vyāpti*-statement. *Viśeṣa* is given conditions of application (*Nyāya-Sudhā* on [*Anuvyākhyāna* 2.3](cite://madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3) *adhyāya*) — when it may legitimately be invoked, what it does and does not commit one to. *Sākṣi* is established as an independent *pramāṇa* (*Nyāya-Sudhā* on [*Anuvyākhyāna* 3.2](cite://madhva/anuvyakhyana/3.2) ff.) by an argument from the irreducibility of first-personal givenness to inferential reconstruction. The *bimba-pratibimba* relation between Viṣṇu and *jīva* (*Nyāya-Sudhā* on [*Anuvyākhyāna* 2.3.43–53](cite://madhva/anuvyakhyana/2.3.43-53)) is shown to require strict asymmetry: any reciprocal construal collapses into one of the positions Madhva's *Upādhi-Khaṇḍana* has already refuted.

Vyāsatīrtha builds the *Nyāyāmṛta* on this base; Rāghavendra builds the *Pariṃala* on it. The traditional saying *yadi sudhā na pīyeta, kaḥ sa-jīvet* ("if one has not drunk the *Sudhā*, who can be said to live?") is the internal-school index of its standing.

engaged_works.title: Tattva-Prakashika on BSB
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Madhva's Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya.
engaged_works.summary:
Jayatīrtha's *ṭīkā* directly on Madhva's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*. Shorter and more closely word-locked than the *Nyāya-Sudhā*, the *Tattva-Prakāśikā* is the principal Mādhva *vyākhyāna* on the *sūtra*-text itself.

Three organisational labours run through it. First, the school's standard division into *adhikaraṇa*-units is fixed here (*Tattva-Prakāśikā* on *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* 1.1.1, the prefatory adhikaraṇa-list). Second, each *adhikaraṇa* is given the *viṣaya–saṃśaya–pūrvapakṣa–siddhānta–saṅgati* schema. Third, contested *padacchedas* (word-divisions, points at which the same string of syllables can be parsed into different lexical units, with very different doctrinal consequences) are resolved philologically.

Doctrinally, the work answers Śaṅkara's reading of the *sūtras* line by line. Four *adhikaraṇa*s are particularly load-bearing. The opening *athāto brahma-jijñāsā* (*Tattva-Prakāśikā* on *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* 1.1.1) is glossed against Śaṅkara's *adhyāsa-bhāṣya*: the inquiry is into a real Viṣṇu, not into a *Brahman* identified with the *jīva*-self by superimposition. The *ānanda-mayādhikaraṇa* (*Tattva-Prakāśikā* on *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* 1.1.12–19) is settled in favour of the realist reading of *ānandamaya* as Viṣṇu, against Śaṅkara's *jīva*-reading. The *aṃśa-adhikaraṇa* (*Tattva-Prakāśikā* on *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* 2.3.43), with its *ghaṭākāśa* (pot-and-space) analogy, is read as articulating asymmetric dependence (the *jīva* as part of Viṣṇu in the dependence-sense) rather than identity-with-modification.

Vyāsatīrtha's *Tātparya-Candrikā* is the sub-sub-commentary; Rāghavendra's further *prakāśa* glosses build on the *Candrikā* in turn.

engaged_works.title: Vadavali
engaged_works.summary:
An independent polemical *prakaraṇa* in which Jayatīrtha argues for the reality of *bheda* outside the commentarial frame. The text considers the principal positive theories on which *bheda* might be reduced and shows in each case that the reduction either fails on its own terms or covertly reintroduces the *svarūpa-bheda* it set out to eliminate.

Three reductions are taken in turn. The Advaitic *vivarta*-account treats *bheda* as superimposed ([*Vādāvalī* §§1–2](cite://jayatirtha/vadavali/1-2), against Padmapāda's *Pañca-Pādikā*): but to treat anything as superimposed requires a real distinction between the substrate and what is superimposed on it, and this distinction is itself a *bheda*. The Bhāskara *aupādhika-bheda*-account treats difference as conditioned by *upādhi* ([*Vādāvalī* §3](cite://jayatirtha/vadavali/3)): but two things conditioned by different *upādhi*s differ by *those* conditions, and the original difference returns at the level of the conditioning factors. The Nyāya account treats *bheda* as a separate *padārtha* (category) ([*Vādāvalī* §4](cite://jayatirtha/vadavali/4)): but on this construal *bheda* itself differs from its terms, generating a *bheda*-on-*bheda* regress, and the Naiyāyikas have no resource to stop it.

The positive thesis is *svarūpa-svabhāva* ([*Vādāvalī* §5](cite://jayatirtha/vadavali/5)). *Bheda* is not an additional ontological item attached to a substance; it is constitutive of what the substance is. *A* differs from *B* in being *A*; *B* differs from *A* in being *B*. The regress-arguments fail because they presuppose that difference is the kind of further entity that needs to be differentiated, and the *svarūpa-svabhāva* construal denies this presupposition.

The *Vādāvalī* is short and is the immediate ancestor of Vyāsatīrtha's *Nyāyāmṛta*-treatment of *bheda* (in the *anumāna-pariccheda*).
